title: Comparatives and Superlatives
subtitle: Comparativos e Superlativos

introduction.definition: Usamos comparativos para comparar duas coisas e superlativos para destacar uma coisa em um grupo.

example.comparative: This room is bigger than that room.
example.superlative: This is the biggest room in the house.

comparative.question: Qual é mais...?
comparative.example: John is taller than Michael. (Comparamos dois homens)

superlative.definition: Destaca uma coisa como a mais... em um grupo
superlative.question: Qual é o/a mais... de todos?
superlative.example: John is the tallest student in the class. (John é o mais alto de todos os alunos)

adjektivRules.title: Regras de Formação por Tipo de Adjetivo

oneSyllable.name: Adjetivos de 1 sílaba
oneSyllable.note: Se termina em consoante simples precedido por vogal simples, duplique a consoante: big → bigger

twoSyllable.name: Adjetivos de 2 sílabas
twoSyllable.note: Adjetivos terminados em -y: troque -y por -i, depois adicione -er/-est

irregularAdjectives.title: Adjetivos Irregulares
irregularAdjectives.note: Estes não seguem as regras normais!

examples: She is as tall as her mother.
This coffee is as good as that one.
They are as intelligent as us.
This house is not as big as that one.
She is not as smart as her brother.
This job is not as interesting as the other one.
This room is less expensive than that room.
He is less intelligent than his sister.
This book is less interesting than the other one.
This car is more expensive than that one.
She is more talented than her friend.
This movie is more exciting than the last one.

examples.correct: She is the smartest student in the class.
examples.wrong: She is smartest student in the class.

examples.correct: This is the most beautiful painting.
He is the best player on the team.
examples.wrong: He is best player on the team.

comparativeStructures.structure: Noun + verb + comparative adjective + than + Noun
There are + more/fewer + noun + in A + than + in B
comparativeStructures.example: There are more students in this classroom than in that one.
The more you study, the better your grades will be.
comparativeStructures.meaning: Quanto mais estudas, melhores serão tuas notas.

practicalExamples.comparative: This apartment is bigger than my old apartment.
practicalExamples.superlative: This is the biggest apartment I've ever lived in.

practicalExamples.comparative: This phone is better than my previous one.
practicalExamples.superlative: This is the best phone on the market.

practicalExamples.comparative: These shoes are more expensive than those ones.
practicalExamples.superlative: These are the most expensive shoes in the store.

practicalExamples.comparative: A car is faster than a bicycle.
practicalExamples.superlative: An airplane is the fastest way to travel.

practicalExamples.comparative: This view is as beautiful as that one.
practicalExamples.superlative: This is the most beautiful view in the city.

commonErrors.error: She is more tall than her brother.
commonErrors.correct: She is taller than her brother.

commonErrors.error: He is the more intelligent student.
commonErrors.correct: He is the most intelligent student.

commonErrors.error: This is the best than the other.
commonErrors.correct: This is better than the other.
commonErrors.explanation: Superlativo é para um grupo, comparativo é para dois

commonErrors.error: She is as smart as him.
commonErrors.correct: She is as smart as he is. (formal) ou She is as smart as him. (informal)
commonErrors.explanation: Formalmente use um pronome de sujeito, mas 'him' é aceitável informalmente

commonErrors.error: This is more good than that.

keyPoints: Comparativos comparam 2 coisas; superlativos destacam 1 em um grupo
Other forms: as...as, less...than, not as...as
